metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Crimson Vow is a "prince format" dominated by powerful rares rather than common synergies. The format plays at a medium pace with strong midrange decks and is heavily dependent on removal quality and managing broken rares. Black contains the densest concentration of powerful rares.

color_notes.{B} Black: Best overall. Highest density of efficient removal (Bleed Dry, Hero's Downfall) and broken rares (Dreadfeast Demon, Toxrill, Henrika Domnathi).
color_notes.{R} Red: Strong removal suite (Flame-Blessed Bolt, Abrade, Rending Flame) but struggles against creatures with 5+ toughness.
color_notes.{W} White: Solid removal access (Fierce Retribution, Sigarda's Imprisonment) supporting multiple archetypes.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Limited hard removal; relies on bounce and counterspells (Syncopate, Wash Away, Geistlight Snare).
color_notes.{G} Green: Minimal interaction; dependent on combat tricks (Wolf Strike) and other colors for answers.

key_gameplay_notes: Splash broken rares with adequate mana sources (minimum 3 sources for 6-turn casting windows).
Avoid double-colored mana requirements in splashes.
Play around sweepers (By Invitation Only, Path of Peril) when ahead.
Hard removal (Bleed Dry, Hero's Downfall) significantly outperforms soft options.
Prioritize proactivity against late-game rares through aggressive early strategies.

mechanics.name: Training (Selesnya)
mechanics.summary: Aggressive mechanic that plays best with low-curve, high-creature-density decks. Pumps trained creatures when they attack with a stronger creature.
mechanics.performance: High variance — requires multiple creatures to set up.

mechanics.name: Cleave
mechanics.summary: Dual-purpose utility spells — pay the cleave cost to remove the bracketed text and get a stronger version.

mechanics.name: Blood Tokens
mechanics.summary: Mana-smoothing mechanic primarily in Rakdos. Sacrifice + discard + draw enables card selection in extended games.

mechanics.name: Daybound / Nightbound (Gruul)
mechanics.summary: Global state — nightbound sides offer significant creature upgrades.

mechanics.name: Disturb (Azorius)
mechanics.summary: Dead creatures return as auras rather than spirits, enabling aggressive tempo strategies.

mechanics.name: Exploit (Dimir)
mechanics.summary: Sacrifice mechanic that creates card advantage through zombie payoffs. Exploit creatures can sacrifice themselves and trigger effects.

ally_pairs.name: Rakdos Blood / Vampires
ally_pairs.strategy: Midrange leveraging Blood tokens for mana smoothing and card advantage.

key_cards.name: Henrika Domnathi
key_cards.type_line: Legendary Creature — Vampire // Legendary Creature — Vampire

ally_pairs.name: Dimir Exploit / Zombies
ally_pairs.strategy: Grindy value deck sacrificing creatures with exploit triggers. Synergizes with death triggers and self-mill.

ally_pairs.name: Azorius Disturb / Spirits
ally_pairs.strategy: Tempo-oriented with fliers and removal. Disturb creatures provide recursive threats as auras.

ally_pairs.name: Gruul Daybound / Werewolves
ally_pairs.strategy: Aggressive werewolves leveraging nightbound upgrades. Benefits from spells triggering day/night transitions.

ally_pairs.name: Selesnya Training / Humans
ally_pairs.strategy: Aggressive human-focused deck pumping creatures through training. High variance due to training's conditional nature.

enemy_pairs.name: Orzhov Lifegain
enemy_pairs.strategy: Cumulative lifegain payoffs with removal and creatures rewarding life gain.

enemy_pairs.name: Golgari Butts / Midrange
enemy_pairs.strategy: Toughness-focused creatures with removal; leverages high-toughness blockers.

enemy_pairs.name: Izzet Spells
enemy_pairs.strategy: Tempo deck with instant/sorcery synergies and cantrips providing card advantage.

enemy_pairs.name: Boros Aggro
enemy_pairs.strategy: Fastest archetype — pressures opponents before broken rares resolve.

enemy_pairs.name: Simic Self-Mill
enemy_pairs.strategy: Only explicitly durdly archetype. Mills for value and graveyard synergies.

broken_rares.name: Henrika Domnathi
broken_rares.type_line: Legendary Creature — Vampire // Legendary Creature — Vampire

general_tips: Splash broken rares with at least 3 mana sources for 6-turn casting windows.
Avoid double-colored mana requirements in splashes.
Play around sweepers (By Invitation Only, Path of Peril) when ahead.
Hard removal (Bleed Dry, Hero's Downfall) significantly outperforms soft options.
Pressure opponents before late-game rares can resolve.
Sideboard aggressively in BO3 — swap low-impact cards based on the matchup.
